summary: I explored the Pipeliner Rails 8 codebase to scope the "Add UI for Dashboard" ask and wrote factual discovery notes to `.pipeliner/phases/01-define/main/codebase-explorer/output/discovery_notes.md`. Key findings: the Dashboard route (`root → HomeController#index`), its view, and the sidebar nav entry **already exist as a stub**, so this is a fill-in rather than a new screen. The three requested panels map cleanly onto existing data — `Pipeline`/`Phase` (status + current_phase enums), `Worker` (online/draining/offline + `last_heartbeat_at`), and `StepRun`/`ManagerDecision`/`Approval`/`ReworkEvent` for activity (there is no dedicated Activity model). Fleet-health semantics are already codified in `StepRuns::Sweep` (2-min offline threshold, role-coverage logic). Reusable UI is in place (`status_badge` covers every status, plus card/table/empty-state patterns and the app shell), and the guides mandate query objects for reporting reads, thin controllers, service-side after-commit broadcasts, and "live by default." I noted the main net-new area is a global Turbo stream for cross-pipeline live updates (existing broadcasts are per-pipeline only), and captured open questions around active-pipeline scoping/user-membership, the definition and window of "recent activity," fleet-health depth, and real-time expectations.
